subject: LU-17400 uapi: Fix incorrect snamelen return value

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Patch Set 1:

I had a patch (that I never finished...) to try to fix this issue: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/52568. Might be worth reading. Huaqing did the calculation a bit differently.

Your patch seems better since you wrote a sanity test. I'm adding some of the people CC'd on that other patch.

Patch Set 2:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> I had a patch (that I never finished...) to try to fix this issue: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/52568. Might be worth reading. Huaqing did the calculation a bit differently.
> 
> Your patch seems better since you wrote a sanity test. I'm adding some of the people CC'd on that other patch.

Thanks for nabbing the needed folks on CC...

Sorry for the potential LU repeat.  I did try searching for the same issue in jira with a couple of different search patterns/topics, but came up empty - figures.

I amended the sanity test after the code commit (the test stuff took me some time to figure out - hopefully I got that right and in the correct "area") and was thrown by the check patch error as I apparently didn't notice that blurb in the ending comment section of the commit amend when I did it.  Now I noticed all the '\0' in my original commit message have been translated to '0', odd.  Apparently the amend requires doubling the \ since they must be eaten by the symlink'd contrib/git-hooks scripts.

I have seen a bunch of these patches along this line. Do we have somewhere in an HDL the format of the changelog_rec? It is easy to miss otherwise

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

(2 comments)

Josh, welcome, and thanks for the patch.

I agree with Tim that this patch is better than the version in https://review.whamcloud.com/52568 both due to clarity, and because it includes a test case.

There are a couple of style issues that could be improved in the test case, and a related issue with the original patch that should be fixed.

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1
> 
> (2 comments)
> 
> Josh, welcome, and thanks for the patch.
> 
> I agree with Tim that this patch is better than the version in https://review.whamcloud.com/52568 both due to clarity, and because it includes a test case.
> 
> There are a couple of style issues that could be improved in the test case, and a related issue with the original patch that should be fixed.

Thanks Andreas, I figured I'd give this one a shot at a fix when I tripped over it looking at a recent/unrelated robinhood issue with the changelog.  I added your prudent safety/range check (sorry I missed the other thread where you had mentioned that concern already) though I went with NAME_MAX over PATH_MAX as it seems to be the choice used at the CR_MAXSIZE define, which makes sense to me if these records are only concerned with file names.  I can push another patch set if PATH_MAX is indeed the correct choice.

parents.subject: LU-17000 lnet: don't assign unused return codes

commit.subject: LU-17400 uapi: Fix incorrect snamelen return value
commit.message:
LU-17400 uapi: Fix incorrect snamelen return value

The sname char array is limited by the struct
changelog_rec.cr_namelen value and has no '\0' character allocated
to it, so strlen() will overrun the char array till it finds the next
'\0' char.

This issue can be seen on the client side when "lfs changelog"
is run and 08RENME record types are present.

Pointer arithmetic was used between sname and name to avoid the
GCC 11 warnings mentioned in 6331eadbd6.

Added Andreas's safety/range check code to changelog_rec_sname.

Fixes: 6331eadbd6 ("LU-15420 uapi: avoid gcc-11 -Werror=stringop-overread")
Signed-off-by: Josh Samuelson <josh@1up.unl.edu>
Change-Id: Ie0817dfdd1d02e06b9399e66f1affaadb9e156c4
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/53624
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
